mapping.a_element: D-brane stabilized at a finite characteristic size
mapping.b_element: spin droplet nucleated with a finite core size

mapping.a_element: quantized worldvolume U(1) flux setting the stable size
mapping.b_element: defect potential strength setting the droplet core size

mapping.a_element: spectrum of small fluctuations of the brane
mapping.b_element: slowly-decaying universal tail of the droplet

brief_justification: The two only share the generic feature that a localized object acquires a characteristic size fixed by a control parameter; the driving machinery differs. Paper A stabilizes a shrinking brane via a Born-Infeld/quantized-flux energy balance (a DBI flux-quantization mechanism with no phase transition), whereas paper B nucleates an order-parameter droplet around a defect coupled to the order-parameter-squared at a metallic quantum critical point (a criticality/Landau-type nucleation mechanism), so no role-for-role governing equation or transition corresponds.
